---
name: Jason S. Tsukahara, Ph.D.
position: Principal Investigator
tagline: Dr. Tsukahara directs the CALM Lab and studies attention control across contexts of life and mind. His research integrates cognitive psychology, neuroscience, and contemplative practices to understand the nature and potential of attention.

image: "photo.jpeg"

department: "Assistant Professor"

office: "Teacher Lab 123"

email: jason.tsukahara@miami.edu
cv: /team/jason-tsukahara/jtsukahara_cv.pdf
website-external:
google_scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=393wE14AAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao
linkedin: ""
bluesky: drjt.bsky.social
researchgate: Jason-Tsukahara
x: ""
github: dr-JT

page-layout: full
body-classes: profile-page

about:
  id: person-about
  template: jolla

listing:
  - id: profile-publications-list
    contents:
      - "../../publications/2023/jepg-squared-intro/index.qmd"
      - "../../publications/2023/ac-psychomotor/index.qmd"
    template: "../../assets/listings/pub-listing-selected.ejs.md"
    fields: [title, pub-authors, show-on-profiles, pub-year, venue, pub-type, pub-status, pdf, data, code]
    sort:
      - "pub-status asc"
      - "date desc"
---

::: {.profile-heading}

# {{< meta name >}} {.profile-name}

::: {.profile-position}
{{< meta position >}}
:::

::: {.profile-tagline}
{{< meta tagline >}}
:::

::: {.profile-links}

<div id="profile-links-dynamic"
  data-cv="{{< meta cv >}}"
  data-website="{{< meta website-external >}}"
  data-google-scholar="{{< meta google_scholar >}}"
  data-researchgate="{{< meta researchgate >}}"
  data-github="{{< meta github >}}"
  data-linkedin="{{< meta linkedin >}}"
  data-bluesky="{{< meta bluesky >}}"
  data-x="{{< meta x >}}">
</div>

:::

:::: {.profile-meta-grid}

::: {.profile-meta-card}
### Position
{{< meta department >}}
:::

::: {.profile-meta-card}
### Contact
{{< meta email >}} <a onclick="copyEmail(this); return false;" data-copy="{{< meta email >}}" title="Copy email" class="copy-email-link"><i class="bi bi-copy"></i></a>
:::

::: {.profile-meta-card}
### Office
{{< meta office >}}
:::

::::

:::: {.profile-section-layout}

::: {.profile-section-card}

<div class="profile-section-icon profile-section-yellow">
<i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i>
</div>

### Education

::: {style="line-height: 0.25; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
[Ph.D. in Cognitive Brain Science]{style="color: var(--color-primary); font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.2rem;"}\
Georgia Tech · Dr. Randall Engle
:::

::: {style="line-height: 0.25; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
[M.A. in Experimental Psychology]{style="color: var(--color-primary); font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.2rem;"}\
CSU, San Bernardino · Dr. Hideya Koshino
:::

::: {style="line-height: 0.25; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
[B.A. in Behavioral Neuroscience]{style="color: var(--color-primary); font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.2rem;"}\
Western Washington University
:::

:::

::: {.profile-section-card}

<div class="profile-section-icon profile-section-blue">
<i class="bi bi-lightbulb"></i>
</div>

### Currently Teaching

[Graduate Courses]{style="color: var(--color-primary); font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.2rem;"}

- Cognitive Psychology

[Undergraduate Courses]{style="color: var(--color-primary); font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.2rem;"}

- Sensation and Perception

:::

::: {.profile-section-card .profile-publications}

<div class="profile-section-icon profile-section-green">
<i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i>
</div>

### Selected Publications

::: {#profile-publications-list}
:::

:::

::: {.profile-section-card .profile-about}

<div class="profile-section-icon profile-section-blue">
<i class="bi bi-person"></i>
</div>

### About

I am originally from Bothell, WA (~30 miles north of Seattle). I will always consider Washington State as my home. I earned my B.A. in Behavioral Neuroscience at Western Washington University. This is where I found my interest in cognitive psychology, Buddhism, meditation, and contemplative practices more generally. I took a year off after college to participate in an 8-week silent meditation retreat on Shamatha and the Four Immeasurables in Phuket, Thailand with my teacher Alan Wallace.

From there I went on to do a 2 year M.A. program in General Experimental Psychology at California State University, San Bernardino (CSUSB). At CSUSB I worked with Dr. Hideya Koshino doing research on selective attention and working memory.

I was, then, accepted into Randy Engle’s lab as a Ph.D. student at Georgia Tech. I graduated with my Ph.D. in December 2022 and worked as a post-doc with Randy for a year and a half. My research in the lab involved investigating the nature of attention control with the use of pupillometry. I made use of the R statistical programming language to analyze data in a way that is most consistent with open science and reproducibility practices. I had developed several R Packages relevant to the lab’s research.

I then went on to do a post-doc with Amishi Jha at the University of Miami in 2024 to extend the work I had done at Georgia Tech in an area that is very near to my heart, mindfulness and meditation. This has been a great opportunity for me to get involved in an area of research I am both academically and personally passionate about.

Outside of psychology, my interests include Buddhist spiritual practice (mainly influenced by the Nyingma tradition from Tibetan Buddhism and Dzogchen teachings in particular), teaching meditation and mindfulness practices, hiking, camping, riding my bicycle around town, and photography.

:::

::::
